steps.title: Identify the structure on each side
steps.content: Left: "...editor Mayra Calvani asked Caridad Pineiro...what" — the verb asked introduces an indirect (embedded) question via what. Right: "Pineiro's response was Wolf and the Dove..." — a new independent clause. The blank completes the embedded question.

steps.title: Apply the rule: indirect questions use statement word order and end with the outer sentence's punctuation
steps.content:
The embedded question is the object of asked, not a standalone question. It must use subject-before-verb order and no question mark.
  • Choice A — was her favorite book!: "asked...what was her favorite book!" — inverted (question) word order and an exclamation mark; both wrong for an embedded clause. ✗
  • Choice B — her favorite book was?: "asked...what her favorite book was?" — subject-verb order is correct but the question mark punctuates the entire sentence as a question, which it is not. ✗
  • Choice C — her favorite book was.: "asked Caridad Pineiro what her favorite book was." — subject (her favorite book) before verb (was), period ends the declarative sentence. ✓
  • Choice D — was her favorite book?: "asked...what was her favorite book?" — inverted word order and a question mark; both wrong. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
